scope: Balancer V2 Vault 0xBA12222222228d8Ba445958a75a0704d566BF2C8, selector-agnostic latest ~100 tx/chain on chain 1 (mainnet) + 42161 (Arbitrum) + 137 (Polygon) = 300 tx. Plus Balancer V3 Router/BatchRouter/Vault probe (chain 1, latest ~30 tx each).
command: KEY=$(grep '^ETHERSCAN_API_KEY=' crates/integration-tests/.env|cut -d= -f2); for C in 1 42161 137; do curl -s "https://api.etherscan.io/v2/api?chainid=$C&module=account&action=txlist&address=0xBA12222222228d8Ba445958a75a0704d566BF2C8&startblock=0&endblock=99999999&page=1&offset=100&sort=desc&apikey=$KEY" -o /tmp/v3diag-balancer/raw-$C.json; done; ./target/debug/v3-harness import-etherscan /tmp/v3diag-balancer/raw-1.json --chain 1 --out /tmp/v3diag-balancer/balancer/corpus.json; ./target/debug/v3-harness corpus --root /tmp/v3diag-balancer

totals.note: covered = swap 0x52bbbe29 only. uncovered = all other Vault selectors, every one soft no_declarative_v3_mapper. Mainnet probe corpus 53/100 matched (= the 53 swaps), confirming.

coverage_matrix._legend: selector -> {name, sig, covered?, harness_verdict, per-chain count in ~100-tx selector-agnostic sample, tier}

0x945bcec9.name: batchSwap
0x945bcec9.sig: batchSwap(uint8 kind,(bytes32,uint256,uint256,uint256,bytes)[] swaps,address[] assets,(address,bool,address,bool) funds,int256[] limits,uint256 deadline)
0x945bcec9.tier: TIER B (cross-array: swaps[i].assetInIndex/assetOutIndex index into sibling assets[]). array_emit.rs names this as THE canonical Tier B fallback.

0x8bdb3913.name: exitPool
0x8bdb3913.sig: exitPool(bytes32 poolId,address sender,address recipient,(address[] assets,uint256[] minAmountsOut,bytes userData,bool toInternalBalance) request)
0x8bdb3913.tier: DECLARATIVE single_emit (request.assets[] and request.minAmountsOut[] are positionally paired flat arrays, no cross-index)

0xb95cac28.name: joinPool
0xb95cac28.sig: joinPool(bytes32 poolId,address sender,address recipient,(address[] assets,uint256[] maxAmountsIn,bytes userData,bool fromInternalBalance) request)

0xfa6e671d.name: setRelayerApproval
0xfa6e671d.tier: DECLARATIVE single_emit (3 scalar args; domain = approval/token; relayer-delegation grant)

0x0e8e3e84.name: manageUserBalance
0x0e8e3e84.sig: manageUserBalance((uint8 kind,address asset,uint256 amount,address sender,address recipient)[] ops)
0x0e8e3e84.tier: array_emit possible per-op, BUT per-op kind enum (DEPOSIT/WITHDRAW/TRANSFER_INTERNAL/TRANSFER_EXTERNAL) needs $match inside element template -> partial; likely Tier B. Not seen in this sample (internal-balance power-user op).

0x5c38449e.name: flashLoan
0x5c38449e.tier: DECLARATIVE single_emit (positionally-paired tokens[]/amounts[]). Not seen in this 300-tx sample (low frequency).

tier_b_required.name: batchSwap
tier_b_required.root_cause: Each swaps[] element carries assetInIndex/assetOutIndex (uint256) that index into the sibling assets[] array. The declarative array_emit DSL resolves $elem against the current element only and explicitly cannot express assets[swaps[i].assetInIndex] (cross-array join). Documented in crates/adapters/mappers/src/declarative/array_emit.rs as the canonical Tier B example.

tier_b_required.name: manageUserBalance
tier_b_required.root_cause: ops[] is an array of (kind,asset,amount,sender,recipient); per-element kind enum requires $match inside the element template to branch deposit/withdraw/transfer semantics. array_emit element template supports $match/$cases, so this MAY be declarative, but the 4-way internal-balance semantics likely warrant a Tier B wrapper for correct domain mapping. Re-evaluate when a real tx is captured (0 in this sample).

new_surface.label: Balancer V3 Router
new_surface.note: LIVE. 4byte-confirmed genuine V3 ops. Entirely new surface, not in registry.

new_surface.label: Balancer V3 BatchRouter
new_surface.note: LIVE, very recent (block 25.2M). swapExactIn = V3 multi-path exact-in swap. New surface.

new_surface.label: Balancer V3 Vault (UNVERIFIED address mapping)
new_surface.note: CAUTION: Etherscan functionName annotation at this address returned inconsistent names (withdrawAll/swapExactETHForTokens/pausePool) and the two dominant selectors do not resolve in 4byte. Either the address is not the canonical V3 Vault or its verified ABI is mislabeled. Must confirm V3 Vault address against Balancer's official balancer-deployments registry (1st-party) before any V3 manifest work.

summary: Balancer V2 Vault covers ONLY swap (0x52bbbe29) today; across 300 selector-agnostic tx (100/chain on 1/42161/137) that 1 manifest accounts for 53/40/1 tx = it covers the MINORITY of live Vault traffic on every chain except mainnet, and just 1% on Polygon. The dominant uncovered functions vary by chain: batchSwap (0x945bcec9) is 88% of Polygon Vault traffic, exitPool (0x8bdb3913) is ~half of Arbitrum, joinPool (0xb95cac28) and setRelayerApproval (0xfa6e671d, NOT in the original target map) round it out. ALL uncovered fns are soft no_declarative_v3_mapper -- zero hard decoder failures, so every gap is a missing manifest, not a decoder bug. Tier classification: exitPool/joinPool/setRelayerApproval/flashLoan are declaratively expressible (single_emit; their parallel arrays are positionally paired, no cross-index); batchSwap REQUIRES Tier B (cross-array assets[swaps[i].assetInIndex]) -- the codebase's own array_emit.rs names batchSwap as the canonical Tier B fallback; manageUserBalance is borderline (per-op kind enum). Separately, Balancer V3 is LIVE on mainnet: Router (0x5C6f...) and BatchRouter (0x136f...) show recent permitBatchAndCall/swapExactIn/removeLiquidityProportional traffic (4byte-confirmed) = an entirely new surface absent from the registry; the V3 Vault address needs 1st-party verification.
